user_query:
Bula! I’m a Fijian women’s rugby player getting ready for trials. Please analyze my last two weeks of training and build a logical, load-balanced microcycle for the next week, then update my plan and draft a message to my coach.

Inputs:
- Data file: input/sessions.csv
- Existing plan to edit: docs/TrainingPlan.md

What I need:
1) Compute per-session metrics from input/sessions.csv and write structured outputs:
   - For each session, compute:
     • session_load = duration_min × RPE
     • meters_per_minute = total_distance_m ÷ duration_min (float)
     • tackle_success_rate = tackles_made ÷ (tackles_made + tackles_missed) for sessions where tackles_made + tackles_missed > 0; otherwise leave blank/null
   - Save a per-session CSV at output/session_metrics.csv with columns:
     date, session_type, duration_min, RPE, session_load, total_distance_m, meters_per_minute, high_speed_m, accelerations_ge_2mps2, decelerations_ge_2mps2, tackles_made, tackles_missed, tackle_success_rate
   - Define two 7-day windows based on the dataset:
     • Week 1 = 2026-04-01 to 2026-04-07 inclusive
     • Week 2 = 2026-04-08 to 2026-04-14 inclusive
     Compute weekly_load for each week as the sum of session_load within that window. Compute avg_meters_per_minute across all sessions in the file. Compute target_next_week_load = average(week1_load, week2_load) (use a floating value).
   - Save a summary JSON at output/summary.json containing exactly these keys:
     {"week1_load": number, "week2_load": number, "target_next_week_load": number, "avg_meters_per_minute": number, "include_speed_session": boolean, "next_week_date_range": ["2026-04-15", "2026-04-21"]}
     Set include_speed_session = true if avg_meters_per_minute < 90, otherwise false.

2) Plan the next week’s microcycle (2026-04-15 to 2026-04-21 inclusive) and save it:
   - Constraints the plan must satisfy:
     • Exactly 5 training days and 2 rest days within 2026-04-15..2026-04-21.
     • Include at least one "skills" session and at least one "conditioning" session.
     • If include_speed_session in summary.json is true, include at least one "speed" session; otherwise optional.
     • For each training day, specify: date (YYYY-MM-DD), session_type (one of: skills, conditioning, contact, speed, recovery), duration_min (integer), RPE (integer 1–10), and planned_load = duration_min × RPE.
     • Total planned weekly load (sum of planned_load across training days) must be within ±10% of target_next_week_load from summary.json.
     • The single highest-load training day must be immediately followed by a rest day.
     • Do not schedule more than 2 consecutive training days without a rest day.
   - Save the plan as JSON at output/next_week_plan.json with an array of 7 objects (one per day) each having: {"date", "session_type" (or "Rest"), "duration_min" (0 for rest), "RPE" (0 for rest), "planned_load"}.

3) Edit the existing document docs/TrainingPlan.md in place to insert the new microcycle:
   - Replace everything between the markers <!-- NEXT_WEEK_START --> and <!-- NEXT_WEEK_END --> with a concise bulleted list for the dates 2026-04-15 through 2026-04-21. Each line should show date, session_type (or Rest), duration_min, RPE, and planned_load. Also include a one-line note stating the total planned weekly load and that it is within ±10% of target_next_week_load.

4) Draft a short email to my coach at output/email_to_coach.txt that:
   - Greets the coach, states that I analyzed the last two weeks and proposes the attached next-week microcycle (2026-04-15 to 2026-04-21).
   - Summarizes week1_load, week2_load, target_next_week_load, avg_meters_per_minute, and whether a speed session is included (and why if included).
   - Lists the two rest days and identifies which training day is the highest load and that it’s followed by rest.
   - References the generated files by path: output/session_metrics.csv, output/summary.json, output/next_week_plan.json, and notes that docs/TrainingPlan.md has been updated.

Deliverables (must exist on completion):
- output/session_metrics.csv
- output/summary.json
- output/next_week_plan.json
- docs/TrainingPlan.md (edited with the new microcycle between markers)
- output/email_to_coach.txt

Please ensure all computations and constraints are met exactly as specified and that all listed output paths are created. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
